messages.content:
Please correct the listed summary comments for progress: Schedule –  Overall production schedule is ahead of schedule contributing from cutting, pre-fab, and unit lift of Unit 10 to mega jig. Hull aluminium cutting is advance on schedule achieving beyond 50% milestone, outstanding is side plate cutting for Unit 06. However, superstructure cutting is now beyond schedule due to delay on cut part drawing release.
Cost – HVAC and Piping MTO materials and consumable runs overbudget.
Quality –Inspection for U10 construction and prefabrication for U01-U05 deck planking and frames completed with minor comments.
Compliance – nothing to report.

Schedule – Overall production schedule is ahead of schedule due to progress in cutting, pre-fab, and unit lift of Unit 10 to mega jig. Hull aluminium cutting is ahead of schedule, surpassing the 50% milestone, with the only outstanding issue being side plate cutting for Unit 06. Unfortunately, superstructure cutting is behind schedule due to a delay in the release of cut part drawings.

Cost – HVAC and Piping MTO materials and consumables have exceeded the budget.

Quality – Inspection for Unit 10 construction and prefabrication for Units 01-05 deck planking and frames has been completed with only minor comments.

Compliance – No compliance issues to report.

Please correct the process update: Schedule –  Overall production schedule is ahead of schedule due to progress on cutting, pre-fab, and unit lift of Unit 10 to mega jig. Hull aluminium cutting is advance on schedule, surpassing 50% milestone, with the only outstanding issue on side plate cutting for Unit 06. Nevertheless, superstructure cutting is behind schedule due to delay in the release of cut part drawing.

Schedule – The overall production schedule is ahead of schedule due to progress in cutting, pre-fab, and unit lift of Unit 10 to the mega jig. Hull aluminium cutting is ahead of schedule, exceeding the 50% milestone, with the only outstanding issue being side plate cutting for Unit 06. However, superstructure cutting is behind schedule due to a delay in the release of cut part drawings.

Please correct the update grammar: Schedule – Delivery targets are within schedule. Aluminium for secondary structure is forecasted to be delivered this month. The delivery includes the one plate (PL05) outstanding for the Unit 06 shell plate which is the cutting is about to complete this week.

Schedule – Delivery targets are on track. The aluminium for the secondary structure is forecasted to be delivered this month. The delivery includes the one plate (PL05) outstanding for the Unit 06 shell plate, which is expected to be completed by the end of this week.

Please correct the progress update: Schedule – Delivery targets are on schedule. The aluminium for secondary structure is forecasted to be delivered this month. The delivery includes the one plate (PL05) outstanding for the Unit 06 shell plate, where cutting is supposedly to be completed this week. 
Cost – Luggage crates proposed costing submitted to RFF for approval.

Schedule – Delivery targets are on track. The aluminium for the secondary structure is forecasted to be delivered this month, including the one plate (PL05) outstanding for the Unit 06 shell plate, where cutting is expected to be completed this week.

Cost – The proposed costing for luggage crates has been submitted to RFF for approval.

Please correct the update: Schedule – IC deliverables are on track. AP deliverables are behind schedule, primarily on fit-out for main deck accommodation arrangement and electrical for main switch boards. Plan approval for machineries and structural from MMD got slower in progress, whereas electrical plans for approval have been submitted last week. 
Cost – all design disciplines are over-budget, primarily to fitout and electrical where recovery plans and overtime are initiated.  
Quality – nothing to report
Compliance – Four special exemptions had been submitted to AMSA.

Schedule – IC deliverables are on track, but AP deliverables are behind schedule, particularly in the fit-out for the main deck accommodation arrangement and electrical for main switchboards. Plan approval for machinery and structural from MMD is progressing slowly, while electrical plans for approval were submitted last week.

Cost – All design disciplines are over budget, mainly due to fit-out and electrical, prompting the initiation of recovery plans and overtime.

Quality – No issues to report.

Compliance – Four special exemptions have been submitted to AMSA.

please correct the update: Schedule – Waterfall chart being updated to incorporate the changes on fabrication schedule to be aligned with design schedule.
Cost –  Variation 001 got approved and variation 002 now under development. 
Quality – Internal NDT plan had been published with 20 RT shots for structural and piping. Sourcing for NDT provider local is being commenced. Schedule for RT timing to planned out. 
Customer Satisfaction – Nothing to report

Schedule – The waterfall chart is being updated to incorporate the changes in the fabrication schedule to align it with the design schedule.
Cost – Variation 001 has been approved, and variation 002 is currently under development.
Quality – An internal NDT plan has been published, involving 20 RT shots for structural and piping. The sourcing for a local NDT provider is underway, and the schedule for RT timing is being planned out.
Customer Satisfaction – No issues have been reported.

Please correct the update: Metal cutting achieve 50% milestone however cutting schedule for the superstructure is now 2-day behind due to delayed release of cut part drawing.
Moreover, the overall fabrication status is ahead on schedule attributed by cutting, frame prefab, and 2-weeks advanced unit lift of Aft tunnel to mega jig.
There is design slack on schedule, primarily on electrical and fit-out where recovery plans are now being initiated.

Metal cutting has reached a 50% milestone. However, the cutting schedule for the superstructure is now delayed by 2 days due to the delayed release of cut part drawings.

Furthermore, the overall fabrication status is ahead of schedule, driven by progress in cutting, frame prefab, and the 2-week advanced unit lift of the Aft tunnel to the mega jig.

There is a slack in the design schedule, particularly in electrical and fit-out areas, leading to the initiation of recovery plans.
how to email your boss for the report you have overlook to submit

Subject: Request for Submission of Overlooked Report

Dear [Boss's Name],

I hope this email finds you well. I am writing to bring to your attention an oversight on my part. It has come to my realization that I overlooked the submission of a report that was due [mention the deadline or timeframe].

I apologize for this oversight and take full responsibility for the delay. I have completed the report and it is now ready for submission. I understand the importance of timely reporting and I sincerely apologize for any inconvenience this may have caused.

Please let me know the preferred method of submission and if there are any specific guidelines I need to follow while submitting the report. I will ensure that this does not happen again in the future.

Thank you for your understanding. I appreciate your guidance on this matter.

Best regards,

[Your Name]

please correct the grammar: Hello Mitch,
I have overlooked the submission of business report for the past weeks. Attached is for last week update.

Hello Mitch,

I overlooked the submission of the business report for the past weeks. Attached is the update for last week.

Thank you.

please correct the grammar: Hello Mitch,
We just now received the quotation from Europa for the Lighting system troubleshooting on Hakuna. I recon we just endorse this them so they could direct them directly. Additionally for the inverter, we received also a quotation on ex-work Canada factory with the cost of USD 995. This could also be endorse to them.

Hello Mitch,

We have just received the quotation from Europa for the troubleshooting of the lighting system on Hakuna. I suggest that we endorse it to them directly. Additionally, we have also received a quotation for the inverter from the ex-works Canada factory, with a cost of USD 995. This can also be endorsed to them.

Thank you.
